codingExamples.title: Print Hello (Assembly)
codingExamples.code:
; For x86 NASM/Linux
section .data
    msg db 'Hello, game console!', 0xA
    len equ $-msg
section .text
    global _start
_start:
    mov edx, len
    mov ecx, msg
    mov ebx, 1
    mov eax, 4
    int 0x80
    mov eax, 1
    int 0x80
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This is a low-level program that prints 'Hello, game console!' to the screen on a Linux computer.
explanation: msg db 'Hello, game console!', 0xA ; Message plus new line
mov edx, len             ; Set length of message
mov ecx, msg             ; Set address of message

codingExamples.title: Add Two Numbers (Java)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This program prints 3 + 4 using Java.
explanation: public class Add {              // Program called Add

codingExamples.title: Add Two Numbers (Assembly)
codingExamples.code:
; For x86 NASM/Linux
section .data
    res db 'Result: ', 0
section .bss
    num resb 2
section .text
    global _start
_start:
    mov al, 3
    add al, 4
    add al, '0'
    mov [num], al
    mov eax, 4
    mov ebx, 1
    mov ecx, num
    mov edx, 1
    int 0x80
    mov eax, 1
    int 0x80
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This adds 3 and 4, then prints the answer as a single character.
explanation: add al, '0'               ; Convert number to a character
mov eax, 4; mov ebx, 1; mov ecx, num; mov edx, 1; int 0x80 ; Print character

websites.title: Network Game Console Development
websites.description: An introduction to building programs for network-connected consoles.

codingExamples.title: Simple TCP Client (Python)
codingExamples.code:
import socket
s = socket.socket()
s.connect(('example.com', 80))
s.send(b'GET / HTTP/1.0\r\n\r\n')
data = s.recv(1024)
print(data)
s.close()
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This connects to a website, asks for a page, shows what comes back, and disconnects.
explanation: import socket                   # Get tools for networking
s = socket.socket()             # Make a connection object
s.connect(('example.com', 80))  # Connect to example.com on port 80
s.send(b'GET / HTTP/1.0\r\n\r\n') # Ask for web page

codingExamples.title: Output Network Ready (Assembly)
codingExamples.code:
; NASM/Linux
section .data
    msg db 'Network console ready!', 0xA
    len equ $-msg
section .text
    global _start
_start:
    mov edx, len
    mov ecx, msg
    mov ebx, 1
    mov eax, 4
    int 0x80
    mov eax, 1
    int 0x80
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Prints a message via Assembly that the network is ready.
explanation: msg db 'Network console ready!', 0xA ; Message and newline

websites.title: A Beginner's Guide to the Computer Console
websites.description: Learn how to interact with the computer console in different languages.

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Python)
codingExamples.code:
name = input('What is your name? ')
print('Hello, ' + name + '!')
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This code asks your name, then greets you by name.
explanation: name = input('What is your name? ')   # Ask for user's name
print('Hello, ' + name + '!')         # Show a hello message

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (JavaScript)
codingExamples.code:
let name = prompt('What is your name?');
console.log('Hello, ' + name + '!');
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Asks your name and prints a hello using JavaScript.
explanation: let name = prompt('What is your name?');         // Popup to ask for your name

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (TypeScript)
codingExamples.code:
const name = prompt('What is your name?');
console.log('Hello, ' + name + '!');
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: TypeScript version that asks your name and greets you.
explanation: const name = prompt('What is your name?');          // Popup for name

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (C)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This code asks your name and displays a greeting.

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (C++)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: C++ code that asks your name, then prints hello.

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (C#)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: C# program that greets you after you type your name.
explanation: Console.Write("What is your name? ");      // Ask name

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Rust)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Rust asks your name, then says hi to you.
explanation: let mut name = String::new();            // Make a string
println!("What is your name?");         // Ask for input
io::stdin().read_line(&mut name).unwrap(); // Get what was typed
let name = name.trim();                   // Remove extra spaces

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Go)
codingExamples.code:
package main
import "fmt"
func main() {
    var name string
    fmt.Print("What is your name? ")
    fmt.Scan(&name)
    fmt.Println("Hello, " + name + "!")
}
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Go asks your name, then says hi to you.
explanation: var name string              // Holds the name
fmt.Print("What is your name? ")   // Ask name

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Java)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Java code to greet the user by name.
explanation: Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);      // Set up input

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Assembly)
codingExamples.code:
; Not practical for collecting input in beginner NASM, so print a message
section .data
    msg db 'Hello, computer console!', 0xA
    len equ $-msg
section .text
    global _start
_start:
    mov edx, len
    mov ecx, msg
    mov ebx, 1
    mov eax, 4
    int 0x80
    mov eax, 1
    int 0x80
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: This Assembly just prints a hello, since taking user input is advanced.
explanation: msg db 'Hello, computer console!', 0xA   ; Message & newline

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (BASIC)
codingExamples.code:
INPUT "What is your name? ", N$
PRINT "Hello, "; N$; "!"
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: BASIC asks you for your name and then says hello.
explanation: INPUT "What is your name? ", N$      ' Ask for name, store in N$
PRINT "Hello, "; N$; "!"          ' Show hello with that name

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Lua)

print('Hello, ' .. name .. '!')
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Asks your name and greets you using Lua.

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Ruby)

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Kotlin)
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Kotlin asks for your name and says hello.

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (PHP)
codingExamples.code:
<?php
fwrite(STDOUT, "What is your name? ");
$name = trim(fgets(STDIN));
echo "Hello, $name!\n";
?>
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: PHP asks for your name and says hello using what you typed.
explanation: fwrite(STDOUT, "What is your name? ");   // Ask name

codingExamples.title: Get User Input (Bash)
codingExamples.code:
read -p "What is your name? " name
echo "Hello, $name!"
codingExamples.beginnerExplanation: Bash script to ask for your name and greet you.
explanation: read -p "What is your name? " name   # Ask and store in variable
